reviews.review_text: Amazing position on the beach but the building is very old and the rooms need upgrading
Good

Very average hotel, needs a good clean , very outdated,
But charging $250 for room key is very un acceptable.
Money for key should be debited once Key is returned to reception.
Told that policy will take around 7 Days, that is very unacceptable these days.
Be very aware of staying here and paying a Room Key Deposit

We stayed in a bright, gorgeous room with amazing views on the ocean and the city, two sides of the room are massive windows, the view was impressive. Great location, friendly and very helpful staff. I can't wait to come back!

Do not book ! They are not focusing on services or solving problems. ||The lady manager ( whoever she is ) is nasty towards customers which you would never see in other hotel! ||Towels are disgusting that you don’t want to wipe your body with  them, as well as the bedding!!! ||There are so many other good options in the area than this one !!!
Positive: Location is good. Negative: I was expecting old, but expecting filthy. Did not feel comfortable sitting on furniture or using surfaces. The place needs a good scrub.

The location is amazing if you plan on going out for the night 200m to the clubs. The rooms however are the definition of "you get what you paid for" nothing actually wrong however quite unimpressive. A good hotel on a really tight budget.

Superb location and brilliant value for money.
We paid $155 for a room on floor 12, with a view of the beach.
Our room was not luxurious, but very comfortable and clean.
Parking available on-site (no extra charge).
Pool, spas, sauna, games room.
Patrolled beaches across the road.
4 min walk to Cavill Avenue and the Sandbar Cafe and Restaurant is on the ground floor.
View is awesome,  rooms are very average for the price, is close to bars and restaurants

This happened today!

If I could will give no stars and remove those falls pictures from the Internet.

I had a very disappointing experience at Chateau Beachside Resort on the Gold Coast. After checking in following an early morning flight, I entered the room and immediately noticed it was filthy and clearly had not been properly cleaned. I was exhausted from traveling and decided to ignore it temporarily and take a short nap.

Unfortunately, during that nap I was bitten by what appeared to be a bed bug. When I reported this to the receptionist, instead of taking the issue seriously, I was asked whether I had actually seen the bug myself, which felt dismissive and unhelpful given the circumstances.

Because of the hygiene concern, I left the room within two hours of checking in, even though I did not have another place to stay arranged. Since then, the hotel has failed to properly respond regarding a refund. I also contacted the booking platform TRIP.COM, but they stated they could not do anything without the hotel’s cooperation.

Given that I was unable to stay due to the unacceptable cleanliness and potential bed bug issue, I believe I should be refunded for both nights of my booking. At the very least, the hotel should refund one night as a gesture of basic customer service. This experience was stressful, unhygienic, and very poorly handled.

I will file complaint to local authorities if they don’t respond by tomorrow.

Adding after hotel management commented -

Let me be very specific. After multiple attempts with Trip, I have screenshots and proof showing my refund was declined over the past two days. As a Diamond member, I pushed for a fair resolution and suggested at least refunding one night. Housekeeping staff are not the right experts to identify bed bugs, please have this inspected by professionals. I have also since discovered multiple additional bites. I will update the review once i receive the one night refund which is partial.

It is an older  building   it is always  clean
Close to beach  restaurant under Neath  food is priced ok   swimming pool. Luv. It
Wonderful location excellent facilities
Location beats everything. Clean and minimalistic.
Great place for the price, Awesome sea views

Perfect location, but rooms need urgent improvement
​The location of this hotel is excellent, the pool is large and clean, and the restaurant downstairs is quite good.
​However, our room on the 11th floor was very disappointing. The beds and pillows were uncomfortable and smelly, and the bathroom and furniture were in poor condition. Cleanliness was a issue, especially the dirty windows and curtains. We tried to upgrade to a better room, but the hotel was full. Great amenities, but the room quality was a letdown.
Amazing beach views from the rooms, great service from the reception. Will come back again.
Bed bug in my room!!!! I escalated and will see how will the management deal with it!

We've been traveling for 6 months and it was good to have our own big apartment. Had cooking facilities and plenty space. Hotel had a small gym, games room, pool, 3 hot tubs, sauna and steamroom. Air-conditioning was pretty bad though
Positive: Location Negative: Paying for extra  towels

Good enough for a short stay on a budget, but pretty in need of renovation.
The hotel is first row with nice views of the beach and / or city (depending on the room).
We had a corner room with no aircon (!).
The staff was very nice and helpful and answered our requests quickly.
The Pool area was nice, the water seemed to be clean. Whirlpool was also nice.
There is a sauna and steam bath, we did not use, but looked ok.

Unfortunately there are still some point to complain about:
The room was quite old with worn out furniture and matress. The room had a kitchenette, but no facilities to use it, beides some cups. Not even dishwashing soap or something. When we asked for some more things, we were give some plates and bowls, but nothing more.
The bathroom really needs a renewal. The shower door could not been slided open / close, as it almost fell out, so we showered with a more or less open door.
The rug in the room was dirty and old. The windows were dirty and some of the window handles were broken due to the salty air.
Hotel is a bit dated but I got it for the good price. Great location. Smooth checkin.
Great location and experience!

Getting charged $250 for a deposit for keys is ridiculous, cost more then our 2 night stay, and don't get the money back for 3 days? windows were dirty covered in sand, couldn't see the view very well that we wanted to see, there was sand in the bed, and no aircon in the room, couldn't watch Netflix as someone had logged in being Chinese and they had a code on their profile which means I couldn't log out, but couldn't read any opinions as it was all in Chinese, and hardly any lighting, the location was great, pool area was good, not clear instructions on how to use the sauna/steamer, and they were empty the sewerage spilling it alot, the smell was terrible, whole street smelt from it, room stayed in was 608, definitely needs alot of fixing and upgrades

Stayed at Chateau Beachside and overall had a good experience. The location is excellent? just a short walk to the beach and very close to restaurants, shops, and main attractions in the Gold Coast.

The room is a bit dated, but it was clean and had all the basic amenities. Staff were friendly and helpful, which made the stay more comfortable.

For the price, it’s definitely good value. I’d recommend it for short stays or budget travelers who care more about location than luxury.

We stayed at Chateau Beachside Resort on the Gold Coast and had a mixed experience.The location is excellent—right by the beach, which was the highlight of the trip. However, the service was disappointing. There was no room service or cleaning, and the room felt outdated and under maintenance. Amenities were also lacking.Great location, but overall needs improvement.
Beautiful accomodation

This was a big apartment with amazing coats views directly across the road from surfers paradise beach. I was on a lower floor but the views still stunning, the price is amazing for location the amenities were basic but no complaints. Also the staff I met were both loverly French people which were very friendly.
It was clean and in a very good location, by the beachside and also a city skyline view at a reasonable price.
rough as bro …

ask for 10th +floor rooms with ocean view.
dont expect clean room but bed was nice and clean. Cant beat the view. The view.
No air-conditioning in the corner rooms aparently. 707 So bloody hot. Glad its not middle of summer.

We stayed on level 12. The only good thing about this hotel was the location, which was very convenient. Unfortunately, the rest of the experience was disappointing.

The hotel itself is very old and poorly maintained. The furniture looked outdated and worn, and the overall room condition was not clean or well kept. Even the blanket on the bed was torn, which was quite unpleasant. There was mould visible on the ceiling, which made the room feel unhealthy and uncomfortable. The lighting in the room was also very dim, which made the space feel even more dull.

Because everything looked old, untidy, and poorly maintained, I found it very difficult to relax or sleep. The couch in the room was also in very bad condition and looked like it needed to be replaced a long time ago.

Overall, apart from the location, the hotel did not meet basic expectations for cleanliness or maintenance.
Friends good service from staff his name Walid good job 👍❤️👍

I was very disappointed with my recent stay at Chateau Beachside.

Prior to arrival I had been assured the room would be clean, however this was not the case. There was visible dust and hair in the room, and when I pulled the couch out it was extremely dirty. The areas underneath the couch and the bed looked as though they had not been moved or cleaned for quite some time, and there were marks on the bedding which was very off-putting.

Throughout our stay there was also a constant sound of water running behind the wall directly behind our bed which made it difficult to sleep. On Sunday morning the cleaning staff began what sounded like vacuuming before 7:30am, which was very disruptive.

Most concerning was that both my partner and I woke up with multiple bites after sleeping in the bed. There were no mosquitoes in the room, which raised concerns about hygiene and pest control.

Customer service was also disappointing. The reception staff came across as very unapproachable during our stay, and when we checked out we were not even asked how our stay was.

I did contact the hotel directly and received an initial response saying the matter would be investigated, however I have not received any follow up since.

Overall, a very disappointing experience.

We just checked in to hotel. They are asking for AUD 500 as deposit and other charges in the case of lose of key or asking for a fresh towel. Initially, I thought the hotel is good, safe and secure; however, while entering to the room, we saw doors are old fashion and not secured well. Sofa covers are dirty, full of stains, and sofa cushions are not good. Glass windows have not been cleaned since ages.

Hygiene is not maintained.

Great location but it’s a mixed bag depending on which room you get.
Our group has stayed there before and been happy with the room, dated but with everything you need. Our most recent stay had us in an unrenovated room with no air conditioning. We kept the windows open at night and the sounds of Surfer’s Paradise was disruptive. There was also a bar fridge when we’d had a full sized fridge in the past. On a small note, there was no dishcloth or tea towel. I asked for them at reception and was told none of the rooms had a tea towel or dishcloth. They do, so it was odd reception was so dismissive.

For the price, the resort has good amenities. There’s a well maintained outdoor pool, three spas, sauna, steam room and a small outdoor play set for smaller kids. There used to be a tennis court but it’s now used for parking.

Inside, there’s a communal laundry and games room. The games room was pretty good but some of the equipment needs to be replaced (ping pong paddles) or is broken (part of the air hockey table). A little bit of upkeep would take it from okay to great.

It’s an amazing location, one street crossing to the beach. There’s also a decent restaurant downstairs.

It’s a decent stay at an incredibly competitive rate but not all rooms will provide the same experience.
The location couldn’t be better, and the rooms are spacious with beautiful sea views. While the facilities appear somewhat dated, I found the price–quality ratio to be excellent!

The room was dated, no air-conditioning, luckily I enjoy the heat, but it did have a ceiling fan. There was some mould in places, like curtains in the bathroom.Would have thought for a 6 night stay there would be extra towels and toilet paper available instead of having to pay extra.Location is perfect amd the pool is amazing.
離衝浪者天堂非常近的海景飯店，有免費停車很棒

Will never set foot in this place ever again and will never recommend to anyone -
My husband had a fall exiting the lift on floor 16 -  after reporting it to reception - the  next communication was from body corporate and we were told that the male it was reported to didn’t recall any conversation.
How convenient, he was the most unfriendly person I have ever come across , definitely in the wrong job.
We never wanted to cause any trouble just a little compassion but Chateau Beachside Surfers Paradise does not know the meaning of the word .
I’m just extremely disappointed and disgusted in the way they obviously treat their guests.
It took me a while to send my review but I have been so angry and gutted , you will never get a single cent more out of us .

Do not stay at this place. Rooms are sub standard. Had a fall coming out of lift on wet slippery tiles when we arrived late at night. Reported it to male receptionist next morning. Did not ask me to fill out incident report or anything. Reported to Body Corporate when I got home who were cooperative and sympathetic to start with, until receptionist denied that I ever said anything, now just ghosting any emails. So guess I am up for all my Medical Costs considering I tore 2 tendons in my shoulder.
To top it off when we left we placed the keys in the key safe as requested then when we got home was accused of not placing keys there and was forced to pay $260 costs for replacement key.
Do your self a favour and scroll on when you come across this Apartment Complex for your Gold Coast Holiday.

The Château Beach resort is located in Central Surfers  Paradise right opposite the beach. The beachfront apartments have obstructed views up and down of the entire coast. There is an outdoor pool with lounge furniture and three spas. The staff are very friendly. The apartments are not modern. However they come with a full kitchen. The accommodation is reasonably priced and the parking is free for one vehicle. If you wish to stay have a late check out it’s only extra $10 an hour after 10 am.
Don't stay here, no AC in rooms, so hot.  They also accuse you of damaging the rooms three weeks after checkout and take the bond from your account, after it was refunded, without notification.
No air con,in the room, they charge 250 dollar deposit for the key which was not 'mention earlier, room was old, dirty, lift were not working perfectly won't recommend any one to stay here
Beautiful views and huge swimming pool

Positive: The hotel is well located near the beach and restaurants. Negative: The room was clean to a certain extent, but the walls were stained and appeared dirty. The fabric couch was shabby looking not sure how clean it actually was. The room just needs a lick of paint and a furniture upgrade.

I have stayed at the Chateau dozens of times over the last 15 years and it never dissapoints . The staff are always friendly and welcoming  , the rooms are always tidy , the hotel is a perfect location - close to everything you could possibly need for a relaxing comfortable stay , and the views are amazing . I have never had any issues when staying at the chateau . It is Great Value for money accommodation without compromising on quality  ♥️ HIGHLY RECOMMEND ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️

Great location. Amazing views. Rooms are quiet. Rooms may look a bit old but have everything you need. Kitchen facilities, comfy bed good water pressure. Lovely large pool and spas. Accross road from Sufers Paradise beach. Walking distance to great restaurants.

Strongly recommend to not stay here if you don’t have too. I stayed here in November 2025 and used the spa and facilities - I then woke up the next day with bites and rashes all over my body. Ever since then, the communication between myself and this place has been horrific. The lack of service and care it’s a big let down, and they keep referring to the “body corp” to get this resolved and get my money back for my stay. If you have to stay here and don’t have any other options, please get fresh sheets and DO NOT USE ANY OF THE FACILITIES LIKE THE SPA. If I was immune compromised, this could have turned out a lot worse. I am still awaiting a respond from this place after sending my medical certificate and providing everything they asked for.
Perfect location for beach, food, beverages and short walk to tram.  Clean and comfortable
The hotel is at the perfect location just infront of surfers paradise.
Dirty rooms , no parking  , images online show a balcony  is really  just a railing infront of a window
Location is the only good thing about this hotel. A 4 night stay and they want to charge for a clean towel. No aircon and the room was dirty

Unfortunately the only enjoyable part of our stay was the fantastic location. This place has so much potential, great size pool!
However the rooms and building are old and ran down making us feel dirty and unclean. One of the PowerPoints was broken/faulty giving a small shock when you touch it. Windows that don’t stay open to let the airflow in with the boiling hot rooms that have no aircon

A bit older, but a fantastic place and people.
Clean and convenient with a nice pool and outdoor spa's. And a sauna. The views are amazing

How do I even start. The absolute worst manager they could have ever had. he is this grumpy old man who constantly yells at you when u do nothing wrong.when u are in the pool area he constantly runs outside yelling at adults and children leaving everyone upset. No children are allowed in the hot tub and no children are allowed to splash around in the pool and have fun.DO NOT RECOMMEND.😠😡 very upset

They didn`t gave me the room I booked - and did not care at all. Old Hotel, very simple furnished (table, sofa, chairs, bed) and very uncomfortable. Cheap and rusty - dirty bathroom and table, sofa - disgusting. Dirt and hair - everywhere! No wellfeeling at all - was happy getting out there! No recommendation - spend some more money to enjoy your time!

Left with a bacterial infection and still waiting for the motel to investigate and refund me from check out a month ago. Its quite disappointing and traumatizing to have left with painful itchy rashes welting all over my body. I have sent proof and they still have not responded nor refunded me. I would not reccomend staying here.
Positive: Location Negative: Bathroom

No air conditioning, no heater, filthy room, and we even found used tissue left behind. Parking was extremely limited and never mentioned before arrival. The staff were rude, unhelpful, and uninterested in solving any problems.

You are charged for everything, including room cleaning, and if you do not clean the room yourself, they charge you. That is not a hotel experience.

The building is very old and worn out, and the only reason they get away with their prices is the location.
Do yourself a favour and stay somewhere else.

Had a great stay at Château Beachside in Surfers Paradise. The room was spacious, clean, and the bed was very comfortable. The location is perfect, right in the heart of Surfers Paradise and close to everything. We had an ocean view room and the view was absolutely fabulous.
The facilities were great too — the swimming pool, spa, and sauna were perfect for relaxing, and the gym and games room were a nice bonus. The room also had a small kitchenette, which was very handy. The staff were friendly and helpful throughout our stay. Overall, a fantastic place to stay and I would definitely come back. 🌊✨

Older building and rooms dated, but clean.
You cannot get a better location though! Directly in front of beach, and walking distance to so many shops and restaurants.
You can even request a smoking room, which is almost unheard of now!
Has secure parking.
Rooms only serviced after 7 days and only 1 towel given per person, you need to hire more for $3 each if they are required. Best to bring your own beach towel and additional bath towels- no beach/pool towels offered.
Pool and spas are lovely, as are sauna and steamrooms. Tge pool is really big and there are 3 spas to choose from, meaning you dont usually have to share with other guests.
Has a large kids games room (costs to play) and a paid laundry to do a load.
Besic, but affordable & convenient location.

Bad experience for children. A man who called himself the “body corporate policeman” approached us at the pool and told our us to quiet our kids at 2:20p because they were loud while throwing a ball. The proceeds to follow them in the hot tub and tell them to stop putting their head under the water. We are locals and were visiting their grandmother here during the holidays.

I have been coming to The Chateau for yrs. New management now but I feel the place was run better previously. Especially with maintaining the facilities like the jakoozi and spa. Room was fine, broken lamp shade and everything so out dated they just don't wish to change anything but make money off it? However, perfect lil hotel right on the beach, immaculate location with all at your feet to see at Surfers. Great pool, gaming room, sauna and spa, jakoozi the Best in GC! From the heating to correct bubble levels it's been my go to. But the plumbing or sewerage was off. Water smelt and my friend visiting was grosed out sadly taking a shower right away. Fix this!
poor customer service. location good

Great location and price. Had issues with late check in, our key was stuck and we were unable to get inside late at night, staff were helpful though and it was sorted within 40 minutes. Room was neat and tidy. No crockery or cutlery provided except two cups, and no dishwashing detergent. We also only had one shampoo given. Great pool and spa.
View from room is fantastic and location too . But toilet door was faulty and no aircon in room. Receptionist at check in should have reminded guest of it when we decide on adding another bed .
Great trivia night with Dan !

I’ve stayed here several times before but this stay was terrible. Our room was filthy from bathroom to kitchen and floors. When I rang reception she was unhelpful and didn’t seem to care. We requested extra parking in booking no one contacted to say that wasn’t possible so we got caught out, what’s point of having a section for booking notes if your not going to read it. My son hurt his hands in some damaged artificial plant near lift. And it’s standard they don’t provide conditioner or a face towel which I found strange. The location/views is great but that’s the only perk here. Everything else one star
Or less
The checkinn was easy and fast the room was clean and cold when i arives so all perfekt i woud to it again

we experienced our first vacation at Gold Coast Surfer Paradise and stayed at Chateau Hotel, which is in a fantastic spot and offers wonderful service. even though we had to pay a deposit, they assured us it would be refunded after three days. we really enjoyed our stay; the hotel and our room were spotless and organized, though it was an older place, it still felt pleasant. And highly recommended and back soon again.

Apartment 910 is absolutely disgusting. Cockroaches, every aspect is dirty! Even when walking to the lifts I could smell the cleaners body odour travelling the halls, i literally couldnt believe the stench as is wafted metres away. Reception would not give me a tea towel when i told them there was not one in the room. No air con....bugs everywhere. This place needs to be condemed🤢🤢🤢🤢🤢🤮🤮

Upon arrival you have a print out from management saying rooms will only be serviced every 7 days,  our guests prefer cheaper rates than room maintenance.|The entire apartment building was in chaos with rooms flooding, showers leaking thru to room below.|Some flooding was almost ankle height. The spa's were filthy,  even green algie in 1of them.|Honestly, this chateau is needing to lose half a Star in ratings.
Positive: Location Negative: Safety. I heard the neighbours fighting outside my door and did not know who to call if some one had actually knocked on my door because no staff around.

I made a simple, reasonable request to mark my passport copy to indicate it was only for hotel use - a standard privacy protection practice in some countries. The receptionist repeatedly called this "strange" even after I asked twice if there was a problem. When I calmly expressed that her repeated comments made me uncomfortable, she became defensive, raised her voice, and accused ME of being rude to her.||When the manager came out, she claimed I had raised my voice (I had not) and that she never called my request strange (she absolutely did for more than 3 times). She even suggested I should not be allowed to check in, calling me "trouble" before I'd even received my room key.||The manager apologized and handled the check-in himself, but both he and the receptionist refused to provide her name when I requested it to file a formal complaint. During my conversation with the manager, she was complaining about me to her colleagues at the back.||I did not ask for or want compensation (the manager offered free breakfast but I refused). I simply wanted accountability and an acknowledgment of what actually happened. Instead, I was denied even basic information to file a proper complaint.||This was gaslighting and false accusations from staff. I remained calm throughout, yet was treated as a problem guest for a basic privacy request. Future guests should be aware that reasonable requests may be met with hostility and false claims about your behavior.||The only positive: the manager did intervene and apologize.||Other than the customer service, I also get shocked by how dirty the public warm spa is, and the photo is attached.

My stay in Chateau is okay. The room is spacious and great location. Just the wall is too thin and I couldnt get a proper sleep whole trip until one night, I need to call reception because my neighbor room was shouting and screaming whole night. I was dead tired every morning because not able to get a good sleep. The rest have no fault.

The location was very strategic, comfortable, the room quite big even though we only booked a studio room, the equipment in the room was complete, clean and neatly arranged, I will definitely come back to stay again

Good customer service - staff at the hotel were welcoming, friendly and very helpful. Location was perfect for exploring the city with all major attractions within walking distance. The studio ocean facing room was spacious, spotless with a comfortable bed. Perfect place for a weekend retreat. Room was clean and comfortable. We appreciated the view of the ocean from the balcony. We had an amazing time during our stay. Definitely looking forward to return sometime soon.
Nice location right in the middle of everything it's not a 5 star hotel, but you don't need it due to its location

I really like this hotel — the location is excellent, and the sea view from the balcony is absolutely stunning. However, the only downside is that the bathroom faucet and toilet brush are very rusty. It would be great if they could be replaced with new ones, as the rust really affects the overall experience.
Excelente

The hotel was overall good, view was really nice only thing is beach side view room don’t have AC, however they have room there with AC, I requested fan and they provided, staff was nice and supportive.Everything was near by.
What an awesome location and value for money. Great rooms too
Needs maintenance. The apartment was rundown.
Location is amazing. And the price is great.
The view is fantastic 🤩

Very satisfied with our stay at Chateau!
We got the 14th floor with an amazing view of the city and the sea.
The building may be a bit old, but it has everything you need (and more) for a perfect stay at a great price.
We’d definitely come back! 🩷
Was shown 4 rooms all filthy, old and horrible. Asked for my money back and left. Place is a dirty hole.
Wonderful place 😁
The rooms are quite old and a little average, bed was comfortable but the extra beds for the teens were a little average

Chateau isn't fancy, the rooms aren't necessarily modern but I give it 5 stars because it's such fantastic accommodation - it's been our go to when my daughters and I want to get away for a 'girls weekend' for the last 10 years or so.  Staff are always friendly, it's in the most perfect position where we can walk everywhere and feel completely safe.  No need to get the car out at all while you're there. The views over the ocean or city are just beautiful.
Very good price for where the Chateau Beachside is . You can see whales from most of the room's. In season

I only visited a friends room who was staying here, and we went to the pool. But this place looks SO worn down, there was mould all over the walls and ceiling in her room and the pool just on a brief look, the bathroom was filthy, the sauna and pool were so unkept and the lifts are barely big enough to get 3 people in at a time! Just from that visit I would not stay there, I couldn't wait to leave unfortunately.
Great place to stay  great access to surfers paradise.
Fantastic food
Our go to choice for accommodation. Close to beaches and central access to everything.

This is a great value 3 star property in a brilliant location.  The rooms are only 3 star but the location is 5 stars.  The pool is nice to swim in and the staff are friendly and helpful.  We always stay here when we go to Surfers.  Great value for money and allows you to spend more in other areas.  Rooms are clean, the views are great. You will not find this value anywhere else in Surfers and if you can handle an old style room without AC you will not regret it
酒店的裝橫和設施都偏殘舊。娛樂室設有收費和不收費的設施，但好像都有點日久失修和髒。泳池不錯，但spa池就有點髒。但總的來說，酒店地理位置很好，旁邊什麼都有，該有的設施也有，服務員態度也很好。所以體驗算是不錯。
There was no air con in our room I would check this in summer. Everything else about it was amazing the view is perfect.

I would strongly advise anyone considering this hotel to look elsewhere. The room was filthy, with dust and grime everywhere, and the furniture looked like it hadn’t been updated in decades. The lighting was so poor throughout the property it felt dark and unwelcoming.||On top of that, parking was extremely limited and a constant headache. To make matters worse, the hotel piles on hidden charges at every opportunity, which left a very sour taste. The staff were unfriendly and unhelpful, making the whole stay even more unpleasant.||The only redeeming factor was the view, but honestly, it’s nowhere near enough to make up for such a negative experience. For the price charged, this was unacceptable. One of the worst hotel stays I’ve had—avoid at all costs.
Was a great location friendly staff but as for cleaning goes it was very unclean

no conditioner or body lotion, have to pay for beach towel or extra towels. room was not very clean/old and very outdated. paid $175 for a night and deposit was $250 - they said they will refund after 3days and then bank may take a week to process it - very bad customer service
Was a nice place to relax for the weekend with close proximity to the beach.
Worst hotel in the Gold Coast. Avoid.
The location really good very accessible , we don’t have good sleep because of the noise outside and the fire alarm every 10 seconds so noisy. The receptionist they are very kind and approachable.
Was a great affordable place to stay. Great amenities and location. Will be back

The location of the hotel was undoubtedly the best part of our stay. It was perfectly situated for accessing all the main attractions, beaches, and restaurants the Gold Coast has to offer. The convenience was fantastic. The service we received was adequate but did not exceed expectations. Staff were polite but not particularly engaging or proactive. Check-in and check-out were efficient, though the interaction felt transactional rather than welcoming. Unfortunately, our room had a persistent, unpleasant smell upon entry and throughout our stay. It was damp and musky, which made the environment less comfortable and welcoming. Aside from this significant issue, the room itself was average—cleanliness was acceptable and the amenities were standard, but the smell was a major drawback.The food we experienced, both from the hotel's restaurant and room service, was satisfactory but unmemorable. It was standard and met basic expectations without offering any standout quality or flavour.

The most DISGUSTING hotel I’ve ever seen. Mold everywhere and so dirty. Even rubbish left under draws, spills over the walls and dust everywhere. I felt dirty even walking on the floor without shoes. I’ve stayed in some rundown places but nothing compares to the grim in this hotel. Would never recommend to anyone. I’d rather stay in a hostel with a shared bathroom as it would be cleaner. I could clean this room for an hour and still make a big difference.

The most DISGUSTING hotel I’ve ever seen. Mold everywhere and so dirty. Even rubbish left under draws, spills over the walls and dust everywhere. I felt dirty even walking on the floor without shoes. I’ve stayed in some rundown places but nothing compares to the grim in this hotel. Would never recommend to anyone. I’d rather stay in a hostel with a shared bathroom as it would be cleaner. I could clean this room for an hour and still make a big difference.
Amazing view loved staying here.

Arrived to find the pool was under maintenance until the end of August. Would not have stayed if known prior. One of the lifts were missing the ‘11th’ floor button. Had to swap lifts at the lobby. Aircon would not work at first when you switched on, 25 mins of gas noises and it trying to force it self on. Last night we had 1.5 hours of the kitchen sink gurgling- it hadn’t been used by us the whole stay. Air vent in the bathroom was covered in dust and mould. There was long black hair in the shower and bed upon arrival. First shower taken we found out there was only enough hot water for one shower- ran out after a minute for the second person. Timed it the second day and it lasted a total of 6.5 minutes!!! Would not recommend to anyone

Stayed here for a weeks holiday with my partner and first night unit directly across from our room had music blaring till 11.45pm which quiet time for the hotel is meant to be 9pm/10pm.Then we come to find out that there is no housekeeping unless you stay 8 days or more,so for a week you get 1 roll of toilet paper.(So obviously then we had to buy our own) Sheets and towels  don't get changed and if you want an extra towel it's a charge of $3 per towel,(i went and brought an extra towel from the convenience store opposite the Hotel,Glass doors to the railing were dirty,Fingerprints on the TV,water pressure in the shower was next to NIL even after them changing the shower head and then telling us that should have fixed it but it didn't,then we got notes in the door to our unit telling us that a real estate was bringing someone through that was purchasing the unit we were staying in (which could have waited till the unit was vacant for this to happen) then what was supposed to be our last night the neighbours music started again (even though it was only early god only knows what time it would have gone on till) we go to reception and tell the woman who's response was "what do you want me to do about it" so we checked out early got our incidentals money back as we had paid in cash but then that amount again had to be held on my partners card until the room was checked the next day and we only got the money back today after a week and 1 day (only because I called them that they released it) This resort really needs to pick up there game and clean the place up and teach there staff better customer service to there guests,I would never stay here again if it was free

The staff manning the reception did their job but not that friendliest. Again, it's not something that worried us. Hi everyone.   We recently stayed in a front room which by standards was going back to the seventies. Reason being the furniture was obviously bought at a op shop.  Ok being the location you accept that.   On booking in you pay a bond of $200 on the credit card.  Fair enough. Especially these days.

Pros - the location is excellent.  Wonderful view of Surfers Paradise beach and to go for a swim you can't get any closer with a patrolled spot across the road.  Price is good and probably the cheapest on this part of the coast.  Free undercover parking.

Cons - no air conditioning in the room I stayed in.  At this time of year it was stifling heat although I knew it had no a/c when I booked.  The lifts are slow and the foyer on the ground floor and on the level I stayed in were stuffy and the heat stifling.  The room needs some maintenance.  Nothing is broken but everything is just old and badly worn.  The floor of the room I stayed in was grimy and unpleasant to walk on.  It needed a mop before I checked in.
No AC so very hot room.
Request a quiet room for a romantic night and ended up booking another night with minimal hardships

The location is awesome, you will not regret that. Even if we did not spend that much time in the room view of the city and the beach was stunning. Friendly staff. The only problem was about the pool, my husband saw a dead cockroach in the pool floating and another one just in front of the hot tub.

Beautiful views along the long beaches from every room in our spacious apartment on the 15th floor. A friendly welcome and efficient check-in. Rooms are comfortable but attention to minor maintenance and cleaning issues will improve the experience. WiFi and TV channels good. Lovely pool area, with hot tubs and sauna/steam rooms. Location is great: across the street to the beach and a short walk to shops etc.

Customer service is awful!

We stayed here during 16/10/2024-18/10/2024. The bathroom door came off its track and the pipe broke at midnight, but the only way to ask for refund is to email them. Even we called them twice after a week of staying, they didn’t apologize and told us to wait for their response. Until today 26/11/2024, no one replied back or provided any compensation.

Pro’s - very affordable for the good location, pool and multiple spas plus the sauna and kids room.Con’s- aircon didn’t work, only free to air tv. Outdated  decor  (expected for the price point of $195 a night in surfer’s paradise)

Location is great and that’s about where it ends, Rooms are dated and grubby, When we booked it was a stated guarantee that our room would be level 8+ when we got here we were put on level 4 (which is fine not a big deal) got to our room it was old and rundown, It was hot and stuffy went to put the A/C on it didn’t work when questioned and ask about it We were told that the aircons in majority of the rooms don’t work, they state smart TV to be able to log in to your own netflix ect, none of the TV’s are able to this apparently, you are charged $3 per towel if you want a fresh clean one. Then to really top it off after using the toilet and flushing it, it just constantly ran, rang reception they told us that some would be up to look at it and nobody came we then went to reception to speak to the staff and were pretty much told to deal with it as nobody was coming to fix it so night all you could hear waa a constant running of water the toilet/bathroom was right next to the bedroom and it was a super annoying noise to try and sleep! Staff were not overly accommodating and were quite dismissive when asked if we could get a refund to book another morning we were told that they don’t over refunds under any circumstances

rooms are a little outdated  staff are friendly  fixed an issue with shower head promptly main issue was the bed was too firm I couldn’t get  comfortable  could feel the springs which made getting good nights sleep not good,  ,the kitchen was fine good size fridge  the only thing I felt odd was there was a working stove top and a toaster   in the cupboard  but husband was informed we couldn’t use either  so  sad to sayI  really missed  having  my morning toast with my cup of tea ,the view of the beach was great there sadly   was no balcony which would  have been nice, apart from the bed our break away  was great the sofa lounge was little too small for 2 unless you are twiggy

We booked this hotel for a 2 night stay and although the staff were very friendly and helpful, they hotel itself was not worth what we paid. When we saw the photos of the hotel online, the rooms looked decent. Nothing too fancy, but still nice looking. When we arrived we were dissapointed. I feel like we were catfished. Very outdated and not nice at all. We tried our best to be pleased and make the most of it, but we couldn't. I'm not usually one to leave bad reviews, but there are some things that need to be said about the quality of the place.

First of all, the aircon was not working. It could turn but for some reason wasn't functioning. It was hot in the room and had to sleep with the balcony window open to let cool air in. We couldn't even turn the fan on high as the fan was too loud and shook alot.
Second, the bathroom smelled heavily of cleaning products, had no ventilations and had rusty ceiling panels and overall looked very outdated and a little mouldy. Not nice at all, I ended up wearing shoes in the bathroom to avoid touching the floor. The toilet seat was also broken.
Third, there was barely any lighting. Aside from the large balcony window (which isnt even a balcony, its just a sliding door with a rail right across it), the lighting situation in the room we stayed in was really odd. No light in the bedroom section, no kitchen light, only a few dim lights in a few corners of the room.
Fourth, the television remote was sticky? It was really unpleasant to hold I had to wipe it down with my own wet wipes. It appeared to have food stuck between the buttons. Just nasty.

Overall, we don't think it was worth what we paid (we paid about $415 for 2 nights plus 200 bond). We aren't a fussy couple, like I said, we werent expecting anything fancy, but it was not at all what we thought we were getting. It felt dirty and not well maintained. Maybe it's just the one room, perhaps other people have had better experiences, but it was not a good experience for us. We ended up checking out early and spending out second night at another hotel that was outside our budget. We were told we would be refunded for out second night if they could sell it to someone else. When we checked out we were told they will refund it, and I am now waiting for the payment to be made back.

On a positive note, again the staff were very friendly and easy to check in with. I trust that they will refund us for the night not spent there promptly like they said. The view was nice and the stay was nice and quiet. A quick walk to the beach and shopping. Location was probably what made they stay somewhat worth it.

I know this review turned into a lengthy one, but I hope they hotel owners or staff take it into consideration so that they can better the experience even for one customer.

Location is good but thats where it stops. The room was hot and humid. No air condition. One ceiling fan in the centre. Had to slide the double bed half across the room to use the fan. There are windows that can be opened but surprisingly very little wind pass through. Also no tv signal as a previous occupier had broken the cable socket. Good side is spent more time on Surfers beach at 9pm as it was more comfortable outside. There is a microwave and a small fridge which functions well. Bathroom is adequate for one night stay. Prefer to stay Beachcomber as a recomendation.

Great hotel for a budget that's right on the beach yet in the town. Perfect location, nice rooms with a view. It would be nice to get room service, even if it's every three days or something. No teatowel, no tissues, no cold water. No a/c, uncomfortable chairs to sit on, otherwise OK.

This is a very good value 3 star hotel

The beach is directly across the road.
The pool ( salt water) is fabulous

My husband and I planned our vacation at Gold Coast, and the hotel was totally not a disappointment.  I read reviews and saw how people were complaining, which made us feel a bit worried as we had already paid, but when we reached and checked in. The views and our room just won us over. Everything was perfect. It was so spacey, with beautiful visits of lorikeets. I loved seeing how the pools were cleaned and every morning  how the rooms were attended to and cleaned by the friendly smiling staffs when guest had checked out. Seeing it myself gave me a relief that indeed our room was taken care of well. I do advise bringing your washing powder and keeping extra cash $200 for key deposits. The hotel is in the perfect location, just opposite the beach and surrounded by many good restaurants. The restaurant Sandbar 🤌 was best. Just imagine good stay nearby good food. Makes a perfect vacation. This eventually led us to extend one more day to vacation. We booked through Agoda next and shifted to a different room. Which also wasn't disappointment. We loved it. Do booked through cheaper price offered on Google's maps. Booking through reception may be costly, which we did earlier. If we knew, we would've paid through Agoda for our 5 days stay. There's a gate from the pool leading to the beach which made it easy to come in and out with wet / sandy clothes. Checking in was fast and efficient. View was great. Good all rounder at a fair price point.
